data.text: I'll start by reading the relevant files to understand the current state before making changes.

input.description: Read key source files for bead implementation
input.prompt:
Read the following files completely and return their full content:

1. /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-a41e6fbb-20260508T012134-beeb3e83/cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_rewrite.go
2. /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-a41e6fbb-20260508T012134-beeb3e83/cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go
3. /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-a41e6fbb-20260508T012134-beeb3e83/docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md (lines 150-250)
4. /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-a41e6fbb-20260508T012134-beeb3e83/docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-023-bead-lifecycle-quality-policy.md (lines 110-200)

Return the full content of each file with line numbers.

data.output:
150	selected a dependency-ready candidate but before DDx claims the bead or
151	creates the implementation worktree. The canonical decision being made is bead
152	readiness assessment, the pre-claim check for tractability and actionability;
153	lint/rubric scoring is the diagnostic pass inside readiness, and post-attempt
154	triage is a separate after-evidence action.
155	The implementation may still call the compatibility entrypoint
156	`PreClaimIntakeHook` and record `MODE: intake` for legacy compatibility, but
157	those names are aliases only. Within readiness, the nested bead-lifecycle
158	workflow skill performs lint/rubric scoring to support the decision, and the
159	score is diagnostic evidence rather than a separate queue action. Post-attempt
160	triage is separate and runs only after evidence exists. The hook has enough
161	context to evaluate title, description, acceptance criteria, labels, parent,
162	deps, bead type, spec-id, prior attempt history, and whether the bead is
163	atomic enough to execute. The hook invokes the nested bead-lifecycle workflow
164	skill under the `ddx` skill tree and records readiness evidence in the
165	layer-3 run record.
166	
167	`ddx work` wires this hook by default in both CLI and server-managed worker
168	paths. Decomposition decisions run with a strong `MinPower` floor, defaulting to
169	the smart/top-power tier floor when no project-specific splitter override is
170	configured. DDx does not choose the concrete model; it passes the raised power
171	floor to Fizeau and preserves any operator-supplied harness/provider/model
172	passthrough constraints. If those constraints cannot satisfy the strong floor,
173	DDx records `agent_power_unsatisfied` and blocks instead of running weak
174	decomposition.
175	
176	The readiness assessment result is one of:
177	
178	- `actionable_atomic` — claim and execute normally.
179	- `actionable_but_rewritten` — DDx applied a validated replacement rewrite or
180	  metadata update that makes the bead more execution-ready before claim. Safe
181	  rewrites may replace noisy, stale, duplicated, or brittle description text
182	  with a fit-for-purpose bead body instead of appending more prompt burden. They may
183	  also format the description into the authoring template, add discovered
184	  file:line evidence, add an obvious test command, or wire deterministic
185	  labels/parent/deps. The mutation is recorded through `ddx bead` paths before
186	  claim, with before/after hashes and enough evidence to audit the replacement.
187	- `too_large_decomposed` — DDx created child beads, mapped every parent AC to
188	  child ACs or an explicit `needs_human` / `non_scope` marker, blocked the
189	  parent, and did not execute the parent.
190	- `ambiguous_needs_human` — the bead/spec is unclear, contradictory,
191	  unverifiable, or missing acceptance criteria that DDx cannot safely invent.
192	  DDx blocks or marks the bead `needs_human` and does not claim it.
193	- `readiness_error` — readiness infrastructure failed. In migration/WARN mode
194	  this fails open with evidence; in reliable factory/BLOCK mode it may skip the
195	  candidate for the current pass but must not park it behind a cooldown unless a
196	  retryable time-based class is recorded.
197	
198	Safe rewrites are validated against durable context, not against a vague
199	"same spirit" comparison to the prior bead text. The prior bead can itself be
200	the problem: it may contain stale line numbers, duplicated history, chat-shaped
201	noise, or contradictory wording. A safe replacement must preserve explicit
202	commitments that remain valid, especially acceptance criteria, non-scope, named
203	files/tests, dependencies, and governing artifact references; it must either
204	carry forward durable root-cause evidence or replace it with current file:line
205	evidence from the repository or governing artifact. The original text is kept in
206	readiness evidence, not copied into the replacement body. Rewrites must optimize
207	prompt fitness rather than raw length: expanding a one-line underspecified bead
208	is safe when the added durable context is required for execution, and compressing
209	a noisy bead is safe when explicit commitments remain preserved.
210	
211	Readiness must reject the rewrite and park the bead as `ambiguous_needs_human`
212	when preservation cannot be proven from durable anchors. It must not invent
213	product behavior, choose between conflicting requirements, change scope, delete
214	unresolved constraints, or guess a missing governing artifact.
215	
216	`PostAttemptTriageHook` runs after the attempt has produced its local evidence:
217	agent result, commit/no-commit state, no-changes rationale if any, post-run
218	checks, adversarial review verdict, merge/preserve result, and the readiness
219	report.
220	It uses the same bead-lifecycle skill to classify whether the outcome is a
221	normal attempt result, a quality-policy failure, a missing-evidence failure, or
222	an infrastructure failure. This is distinct from bead readiness assessment and
223	from the lint/rubric scoring used inside readiness. The hook must never rewrite
224	the attempt result or erase artifacts; it only adds triage evidence and feeds
225	retry/stop classification.
226	
227	If post-attempt triage finds that an implementation attempt stopped because the
228	bead was too large or the worker could not legally decompose inside its
229	worktree/depth context, the layer-3 worker must invoke the same orchestrator
230	decomposition path used by `BeadReadinessHook`. This is machine-actionable
231	work: DDx files child beads, records the AC map, and blocks the parent unless
232	the split is lossy, ambiguous, or at the queue-level decomposition depth cap.
233	The operator is not required merely because the implementer could not split
234	from inside the attempted execution.
235	
236	`ExecuteBeadReport` gains `OutcomeReason` beside the existing `Disrupted`
237	signal. `Disrupted` remains the mechanical indicator that normal completion was
238	interrupted. `OutcomeReason` is the stable classification string that explains
239	why the attempt ended, such as `lint_warn`, `lint_blocked`,
240	`lint_hook_error_fail_open`, `readiness_ambiguous`, `readiness_decomposed`,
241	`triage_missing_evidence`, `review_fixable_gap`, `review_spec_gap`,
242	`review_too_large`, `post_run_check_failed`, or `success`. Layer-3 `ddx work`
243	records aggregate these reasons when evaluating retry eligibility, no-progress,
244	blocked, and deferred stop conditions.
245	
246	The hook evidence lives in the attempt evidence bundle and is surfaced through
247	the unified run substrate. The originating bead may receive an evidence event
248	only for explicit operator override (`--force --reason`) or other existing
249	audit events; lint scores are not copied into `.ddx/beads.jsonl`.

110	  criteria through children, and pure deletion or rename beads may omit
111	  criterion (d) while preserving behavior.
112	- **Per-bead labels.** Rare exceptions use labels of the form
113	  `lint-waiver:<criterion>`, for example `lint-waiver:c`. Labels are the
114	  durable waiver store because FEAT-004 already defines labels as free-form
115	  bead metadata and no schema change is warranted.
116	
117	Operator override uses `--force --reason <text>`. The override records an event
118	in the bead evidence stream naming the criterion, mode, actor, reason, and lint
119	summary. It does not silently bypass the hook and it does not mutate the rubric.
120	
121	### Fail-Open Behavior
122	
123	The lifecycle hooks follow reliability principle P1's fail-open posture for
124	infrastructure failures.
125	
126	- If the hook cannot run, the workflow skill is missing, output cannot be
127	  parsed, or evidence cannot be written, DDx records the infrastructure failure
128	  and proceeds.
129	- In WARN-ONLY mode, all valid lint results proceed after reporting.
130	- In BLOCK mode, only a valid post-waiver lint result below the configured
131	  threshold blocks dispatch.
132	- Post-attempt triage failures never erase the attempt result. They annotate the
133	  report and evidence so retry policy and operators can distinguish "agent made
134	  a bad attempt" from "quality infrastructure failed."
135	
136	### Safe Improvement, Decomposition, And Recovery UX
137	
138	Bead readiness assessment may update a bead before execution only when the
139	update is intent-preserving and grounded in durable context. Safe improvements
140	include validated replacement rewrites of the description, normalizing the bead
141	body into the authoring template, adding discovered file:line evidence, adding
142	an obvious subsystem test command, and wiring deterministic labels, parent, or
143	dependencies.
144	
145	Validated replacement is preferred over append-only amendment when replacement
146	would make the bead a better implementation prompt. Prompt fitness is part of
147	the quality policy: readiness should leave the next agent enough durable context
148	to execute without dragging along stale, duplicated, or chat-shaped noise. A
149	one-line vague bead may need to expand into a standalone task; a noisy bead may
150	need to shrink. The original bead text remains auditable through the readiness
151	event and before/after hashes; it does not have to be embedded verbatim in the
152	replacement prompt body.
153	
154	The validator proves preservation from durable anchors rather than asking
155	whether the new bead is merely "in the spirit" of the old one. Acceptance
156	criteria, non-scope, governing artifact references, dependencies, named
157	files/tests, and still-valid root-cause evidence are explicit commitments and
158	must be preserved or replaced with current durable evidence. If the old bead
159	contains contradictions, stale anchors, or missing product choices, readiness may
160	remove the confusing wording only when the replacement clearly marks the
161	unresolved part as `needs_human` or preserves it as an explicit constraint.
162	Readiness must block for human input instead of inventing acceptance criteria,
163	changing scope, choosing between conflicting requirements, deleting unresolved
164	constraints, or guessing a missing governing artifact.
165	
166	If readiness finds the bead too broad, it decomposes before claim. Every parent AC
167	must map to at least one child AC or be explicitly marked `needs_human` or
168	`non_scope`; token-overlap metrics are heuristics, not proof of preservation.
169	The parent is blocked/decomposed with child ids and the AC map in evidence.
170	
171	When BLOCK mode stops dispatch, the operator-facing output must be actionable.
172	It prints:
173	
174	- bead id, score, mode, and blocked criteria
175	- the missing fields or malformed sections in plain language
176	- the waiver labels already applied and the labels that would waive each
177	  failed criterion
178	- suggested `ddx bead update` commands for adding description, acceptance,
179	  labels, parent, notes, or dependency metadata
180	- the `--force --reason` form for exceptional dispatch, with the note that an
181	  evidence event will be recorded
182	
183	Recovery output must not require reading the lint implementation or an
184	out-of-band report to fix an ordinary authoring problem.
185	
186	## Consequences
187	
188	- FEAT-004 owns the label-based waiver storage and the no-new-schema rule.
189	- FEAT-010 owns hook insertion in `ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime`, attempt
190	  classification, and the `OutcomeReason` report field.
191	- FEAT-011 owns nested workflow-skill packaging so the same rubric guidance can
192	  be reused by lint, triage, review, breakdown, replay, and benchmark flows.
193	- Legacy beads can continue running in WARN-ONLY while the queue is baselined.
194	- Once BLOCK mode is enabled, dispatch quality becomes explicit and auditable
195	  instead of depending on operator memory.
196	
197	## Non-Goals
198	
199	- A new FEAT for bead quality; the existing feature specs own the affected

1	package agent
2	
3	import (
4		"bytes"
5		"context"
6		"encoding/json"
7		"errors"
8		"fmt"
9		"strings"
10		"sync/atomic"
11		"testing"
12		"time"
13	
14		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
15		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"
16		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
17		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
18	)
19	
20	func TestIntake_ActionableAtomic_ClaimsNormally(t *testing.T) {
21		inner, candidate, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
22		store := &claimCountingStore{Store: inner}
23	
24		var intakeCalls int32
25		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
26			Store: store,
27			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
28				return ExecuteBeadReport{
29					BeadID:    beadID,
30					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
31					SessionID: "sess-intake",
32					ResultRev: "abc123",
33				}, nil
34			}),
35		}
36	
37		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
38		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
39	
40		result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
41			Once: true,
42			PreClaimIntakeHook: func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (PreClaimIntakeResult, error) {
43				atomic.AddInt32(&intakeCalls, 1)
44				return PreClaimIntakeResult{Outcome: PreClaimIntakeActionableAtomic}, nil
45			},
46		})
47		require.NoError(t, err)
48		require.NotNil(t, result)
49	
50		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), atomic.LoadInt32(&intakeCalls), "intake hook must run once")
51		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), atomic.LoadInt32(&store.claimCalls), "actionable atomic intake must proceed to Claim")
52		assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Attempts)
53		assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Successes)
54	
55		got, err := inner.Get(candidate.ID)
56		require.NoError(t, err)
57		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusClosed, got.Status)
58	}
59	
60	func TestIntake_InfrastructureErrorFailsOpen(t *testing.T) {
61		inner, _, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
62		store := &claimCountingStore{Store: inner}
63	
64		var eventSink bytes.Buffer
65		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
66			Store: store,
67			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
68				return ExecuteBeadReport{
69					BeadID:    beadID,
70					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
71					SessionID: "sess-intake-warn",
72					ResultRev: "def456",
73				}, nil
74			}),
75		}
76	
77		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
78		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
79	
80		result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
81			Once:      true,
82			EventSink: &eventSink,
83			PreClaimIntakeHook: func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (PreClaimIntakeResult, error) {
84				return PreClaimIntakeResult{}, fmt.Errorf("intake service unavailable")
85			},
86		})
87		require.NoError(t, err)
88		require.NotNil(t, result)
89	
90		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), atomic.LoadInt32(&store.claimCalls), "intake infra errors must fail open and continue to Claim")
91		assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Attempts)
92		assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Successes)
93		assert.Contains(t, eventSink.String(), "pre_claim_intake.warn")
94		assert.Contains(t, eventSink.String(), "intake_error")
95		assert.Contains(t, eventSink.String(), "intake service unavailable")
96	}
97	
98	func TestIntake_HookRunsAfterClaim(t *testing.T) {
99		inner, _, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
100		var preflightSeen int32
101		var intakeSeen int32
102		store := &claimCountingStore{
103			Store: inner,
104			beforeClaim: func() {
105				if atomic.LoadInt32(&preflightSeen) == 0 {
106					t.Fatal("route preflight must run before Claim")
107				}
108				// Claim now happens BEFORE intake: intake must NOT have run yet.
109				if atomic.LoadInt32(&intakeSeen) != 0 {
110					t.Fatal("Claim must run before PreClaimIntakeHook")
111				}
112			},
113		}
114	
115		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
116			Store: store,
117			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
118				return ExecuteBeadReport{
119					BeadID:    beadID,
120					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
121					SessionID: "sess-intake-order",
122					ResultRev: "fedcba",
123				}, nil
124			}),
125		}
126	
127		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker", Harness: "claude", Model: "claude-sonnet-4-6"}
128		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
129	
130		result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
131			Once: true,
132			RoutePreflight: func(ctx context.Context, harness, model string) error {
133				atomic.StoreInt32(&preflightSeen, 1)
134				return nil
135			},
136			PreClaimIntakeHook: func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (PreClaimIntakeResult, error) {
137				if atomic.LoadInt32(&preflightSeen) == 0 {
138					t.Fatal("PreClaimIntakeHook must run after route preflight")
139				}
140				if atomic.LoadInt32(&store.claimCalls) == 0 {
141					t.Fatal("PreClaimIntakeHook must run after Claim")
142				}
143				atomic.StoreInt32(&intakeSeen, 1)
144				return PreClaimIntakeResult{Outcome: PreClaimIntakeActionableAtomic}, nil
145			},
146		})
147		require.NoError(t, err)
148		require.NotNil(t, result)
149	
150		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), atomic.LoadInt32(&preflightSeen), "route preflight must run")
151		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), atomic.LoadInt32(&intakeSeen), "intake hook must run")
152		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), atomic.LoadInt32(&store.claimCalls), "Claim must run before intake")
153	}
154	
155	func TestIntake_NonAtomicSkipsClaim(t *testing.T) {
156		inner, _, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
157		store := &claimCountingStore{Store: inner}
158	
159		var intakeCalls int32
160		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
161			Store: store,
162			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
163				t.Fatal("executor must not run when intake is non-atomic")
164				return ExecuteBeadReport{}, nil
165			}),
166		}
167	
168		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
169		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
170	
171		result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
172			Once: true,
173			PreClaimIntakeHook: func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (PreClaimIntakeResult, error) {
174				atomic.AddInt32(&intakeCalls, 1)
175				return PreClaimIntakeResult{
176					Outcome: PreClaimIntakeAmbiguousNeedsHuman,
177					Detail:  "need human clarification",
178				}, nil
179			},
180		})
181		require.NoError(t, err)
182		require.NotNil(t, result)
183	
184		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), atomic.LoadInt32(&intakeCalls))
185		// Claim now happens before intake; for terminal outcomes the bead is
186		// claimed then immediately unclaimed, so claimCalls == 1 but the bead
187		// returns to StatusOpen and execution does not proceed.
188		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), atomic.LoadInt32(&store.claimCalls), "claim must run before intake check")
189		assert.Equal(t, 0, result.Attempts)
190	
191		got, err := inner.Get("ddx-0001")
192		require.NoError(t, err)
193		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, got.Status)
194	}

196	func TestIntake_ErrorContinuesToClaimWithoutParkingCandidate(t *testing.T) {
197		inner, candidate, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
198		store := &claimCountingStore{Store: inner}

201		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
202			Store: store,
203			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
204				return ExecuteBeadReport{

209				}, nil
210			}),

214		}
215

244	func TestIntake_LogsStartBeforeHookReturns(t *testing.T) {
245		inner, candidate, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
246		store := &claimCountingStore{Store: inner}
247	
248		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
249			Store: store,
250			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
251				return ExecuteBeadReport{
252					BeadID:    beadID,
253					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
254					SessionID: "sess-intake-start",
255					ResultRev: "abc789",
256				}, nil
257			}),
258		}
259	
260		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
261		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
262	
263		started := make(chan struct{})
264		release := make(chan struct{})
265		done := make(chan error, 1)
266		var log bytes.Buffer
267		var eventSink bytes.Buffer
268	
269		go func() {
270			_, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
271				Once:      true,
272				Log:       &log,
273				EventSink: &eventSink,
274				PreClaimIntakeHook: func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (PreClaimIntakeResult, error) {
275					close(started)
276					<-release
277					return PreClaimIntakeResult{Outcome: PreClaimIntakeActionableAtomic}, nil
278				},
279			})
280			done <- err
281		}()
282	
283		select {
284		case <-started:
285		case <-time.After(2 * time.Second):
286			t.Fatal("intake hook did not start")
287		}
288		assert.Contains(t, log.String(), "pre-claim intake: starting "+candidate.ID)
289		assert.Contains(t, eventSink.String(), "pre_claim_intake.start")
290	
291		close(release)
292		require.NoError(t, <-done)
293		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), atomic.LoadInt32(&store.claimCalls))
294	}

296	func TestIntake_ActionableButRewritten_UpdatesAfterClaim(t *testing.T) {
297		inner, candidate, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
298		require.NoError(t, inner.Update(candidate.ID, func(b *bead.Bead) {
299			b.Description = "PROBLEM\nlegacy intake is vague\n\nROOT CAUSE\nmissing constraints\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nrewrite the bead\n\nNON-SCOPE\nchange no product semantics\n"
300			b.Acceptance = "1. preserve the original intent\n2. name the verification command"
301		}))
302	
303		var claimSeenBeforeRewrite int32
304		store := &claimCountingStore{
305			Store: inner,
306			beforeClaim: func() {
307				// Claim now happens BEFORE intake, so the description must NOT
308				// be rewritten yet when beforeClaim is called.
309				got, err := inner.Get(candidate.ID)
310				require.NoError(t, err)
311				if strings.Contains(got.Description, "Add an explicit validation step.") {
312					t.Fatalf("description must NOT be rewritten before Claim; got %q", got.Description)
313				}
314				atomic.StoreInt32(&claimSeenBeforeRewrite, 1)
315			},
316		}
317	
318		var eventSink bytes.Buffer
319		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
320			Store: store,
321			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
322				return ExecuteBeadReport{
323					BeadID:    beadID,
324					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
325					SessionID: "sess-intake-rewrite",
326					ResultRev: "abc123",
327				}, nil
328			}),
329		}
330	
331		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
332		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
333	
334		result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
335			Once:      true,
336			EventSink: &eventSink,
337			PreClaimIntakeHook: func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (PreClaimIntakeResult, error) {
338				return PreClaimIntakeResult{
339					Outcome: PreClaimIntakeActionableButRewritten,
340					Detail:  "tightened scope and verification",
341					Rewrite: PreClaimIntakeRewrite{
342						Description:   "PROBLEM\nlegacy intake is vague\n\nROOT CAUSE\nmissing constraints\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nrewrite the bead\n\nNON-SCOPE\nchange no product semantics\n\nAdd an explicit validation step.",
343						Acceptance:    "1. preserve the original intent\n2. name the verification command\n3. lefthook run pre-commit",
344						ChangedFields: []string{"description", "acceptance"},
345					},
346				}, nil
347			},
348		})
349		require.NoError(t, err)
350		require.NotNil(t, result)
351	
352		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), atomic.LoadInt32(&store.claimCalls), "rewritten intake must still proceed to Claim")
353		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), atomic.LoadInt32(&claimSeenBeforeRewrite), "claim must happen before rewrite")
354		assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Attempts)
355		assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Successes)
356	
357		got, err := inner.Get(candidate.ID)
358		require.NoError(t, err)
359		assert.Contains(t, got.Description, "Add an explicit validation step.")
360		assert.Contains(t, got.Acceptance, "lefthook run pre-commit")
361	
362		events, err := inner.Events(candidate.ID)
363		require.NoError(t, err)
364		var found bool
365		for _, ev := range events {
366			if ev.Kind != "intake-rewritten" {
367				continue
368			}
369			found = true
370			assert.Contains(t, ev.Summary, "description")
371			assert.Contains(t, ev.Summary, "acceptance")
372			var body map[string]any
373			require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(ev.Body), &body))
374			assert.Equal(t, "tightened scope and verification", body["rationale"])
375			assert.Contains(t, body, "before")
376			assert.Contains(t, body, "after")
377			assert.NotContains(t, ev.Body, "MODE: intake")
378			assert.NotContains(t, ev.Body, "legacy intake is vague\n\nROOT CAUSE")
379		}
380		require.True(t, found, "rewritten intake must record an intake-rewritten event")
381	}
382	
383	func TestIntake_UnsafeRewriteBlocksForHuman(t *testing.T) {
384		inner, candidate, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
385		require.NoError(t, inner.Update(candidate.ID, func(b *bead.Bead) {
386			b.Description = "PROBLEM\nlegacy intake is vague\n\nROOT CAUSE\nmissing constraints\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nrewrite the bead\n"
387			b.Acceptance = "1. preserve the original intent\n2. name the verification command"
388		}))
389	
390		store := &claimCountingStore{Store: inner}
391		var eventSink bytes.Buffer
392		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
393			Store: store,
394			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
395				t.Fatal("executor must not run for unsafe rewrites")
396				return ExecuteBeadReport{}, nil
397			}),
398		}
399	
400		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
401		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
402	
403		result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
404			Once:      true,
405			EventSink: &eventSink,
406			PreClaimIntakeHook: func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (PreClaimIntakeResult, error) {
407				return PreClaimIntakeResult{
408					Outcome: PreClaimIntakeActionableButRewritten,
409					Detail:  "attempted to drop acceptance criteria",
410					Rewrite: PreClaimIntakeRewrite{
411						Description:   "PROBLEM\ninvented product semantics\n",
412						Acceptance:    "1. preserve the original intent",
413						ChangedFields: []string{"description", "acceptance"},
414					},
415				}, nil
416			},
417		})
418		require.NoError(t, err)
419		require.NotNil(t, result)
420	
421		// Claim now happens before intake; for unsafe rewrites the bead is
422		// claimed then immediately unclaimed, so claimCalls == 1 but execution
423		// does not proceed and the bead description/acceptance must be unchanged.
424		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), atomic.LoadInt32(&store.claimCalls), "claim runs before intake check")
425		assert.Contains(t, eventSink.String(), "pre_claim_intake.blocked")
426		assert.Contains(t, eventSink.String(), "ambiguous_needs_human")
427		assert.Equal(t, 0, result.Attempts)
428	
429		got, err := inner.Get(candidate.ID)
430		require.NoError(t, err)
431		assert.NotContains(t, got.Description, "invented product semantics")
432		assert.Equal(t, "1. preserve the original intent\n2. name the verification command", got.Acceptance)
433	}

1	package agent
2	
3	import "context"
4	
5	// PreClaimIntakeOutcome classifies the pre-claim intake decision for a bead.
6	// Only actionable_atomic proceeds directly to Claim; intake_error fail-opens.
7	type PreClaimIntakeOutcome string
8	
9	const (
10		PreClaimIntakeActionableAtomic       PreClaimIntakeOutcome = "actionable_atomic"
11		PreClaimIntakeActionableButRewritten PreClaimIntakeOutcome = "actionable_but_rewritten"
12		PreClaimIntakeTooLargeDecomposed     PreClaimIntakeOutcome = "too_large_decomposed"
13		PreClaimIntakeAmbiguousNeedsHuman    PreClaimIntakeOutcome = "ambiguous_needs_human"
14		PreClaimIntakeError                  PreClaimIntakeOutcome = "intake_error"
15	)
16	
17	// PreClaimIntakeResult is the typed decision returned by the intake hook.
18	// Detail carries the human-readable rationale or infra failure context.
19	type PreClaimIntakeResult struct {
20		Outcome PreClaimIntakeOutcome `json:"outcome,omitempty"`
21		Detail  string                `json:"detail,omitempty"`
22		Rewrite PreClaimIntakeRewrite `json:"rewrite,omitempty"`
23	}
24	
25	// PreClaimIntakeRewrite carries intent-preserving bead edits returned by the
26	// intake hook when a bead is actionable but needs safe refinement before
27	// claim.
28	type PreClaimIntakeRewrite struct {
29		Description   string   `json:"description,omitempty"`
30		Acceptance    string   `json:"acceptance,omitempty"`
31		ChangedFields []string `json:"changed_fields,omitempty"`
32	}
33	
34	// PreClaimIntakeHook classifies a ready bead before Claim. The loop treats
35	// actionable_atomic as pass-through, skips Claim for non-atomic outcomes, and
36	// fail-opens when the hook returns an infrastructure error.
37	type PreClaimIntakeHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (PreClaimIntakeResult, error)
38	
39	func (r PreClaimIntakeResult) normalizedOutcome() PreClaimIntakeOutcome {
40		switch r.Outcome {
41		case "", PreClaimIntakeActionableAtomic:
42			return PreClaimIntakeActionableAtomic
43		case PreClaimIntakeActionableButRewritten, PreClaimIntakeTooLargeDecomposed,
44			PreClaimIntakeAmbiguousNeedsHuman, PreClaimIntakeError:
45			return r.Outcome
46		default:
47			return PreClaimIntakeError
48		}
49	}
50

20	type preClaimIntakeHookServiceStub struct {
21		executeCalls int32
22		lastReq      agentlib.ServiceExecuteRequest
23		listModels   []agentlib.ModelInfo
24		executeErr   error
25		executeFunc  func(agentlib.ServiceExecuteRequest) (<-chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, error)
26		finalText    string
27	}
28	
29	func (s *preClaimIntakeHookServiceStub) Execute(_ context.Context, req agentlib.ServiceExecuteRequest) (<-chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, error) {
30		atomic.AddInt32(&s.executeCalls, 1)
31		s.lastReq = req
32		if s.executeFunc != nil {
33			return s.executeFunc(req)
34		}
35		if s.executeErr != nil {
36			return nil, s.executeErr
37		}
38		text := s.finalText
39		if text == "" {
40			text = `{"classification":"atomic","confidence":0.99,"reasoning":"ready"}`
41		}
42		ch := make(chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, 1)
43		ch <- agentlib.ServiceEvent{Type: "final", Data: []byte(`{"status":"success","final_text":` + fmt.Sprintf("%q", text) + `}`)}
44		close(ch)
45		return ch, nil
46	}
47	
48	func (s *preClaimIntakeHookServiceStub) ResolveRoute(_ context.Context, _ agentlib.RouteRequest) (*agentlib.RouteDecision, error) {
49		return nil, fmt.Errorf("ResolveRoute should not be called in intake hook tests")
50	}

54		close(ch)
55		return ch, nil
56	}

110	func newPreClaimIntakeHookTestRoot(t *testing.T) string {
111		t.Helper()
112		root := t.TempDir()
113		skillDir := filepath.Join(root, ".agents", "skills", "ddx", "bead-lifecycle")
114		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(skillDir, 0o755))
115		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(skillDir, "SKILL.md"), []byte("intake"), 0o644))
116		return root
117	}
118	
119	func newPreClaimIntakeHookTestStore(t *testing.T, root string) (*bead.Store, *bead.Bead) {
120		t.Helper()
121		store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(root, ".ddx"))
122		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
123		b := &bead.Bead{
124			ID:          "ddx-intake-1",
125			Title:       "work: wire strong-model intake decomposition into ddx work",
126			IssueType:   bead.DefaultType,
127			Status:      bead.StatusOpen,
128			Priority:    1,
129			Description: "PROBLEM\nmissing intake\n\nROOT CAUSE\nno hook\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nadd hook\n",
130			Acceptance:  "1. TestDecompositionHook_UsesStrongMinPower\n2. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green\n3. lefthook run pre-commit passes",
131			Labels:      []string{"phase:2", "area:agent", "kind:feature"},
132		}
133		require.NoError(t, store.Create(b))
134		return store, b
135	}

138		cfg := config.NewTestConfigForRun(config.TestRunConfigOpts{

140		})
141		return cfg.Resolve(config.CLIOverrides{Harness: "claude"})
142	}

144	func TestDecompositionHook_UsesStrongMinPower(t *testing.T) {
145		root := newPreClaimIntakeHookTestRoot(t)
146		store, b := newPreClaimIntakeHookTestStore(t, root)
147	
148		svc := &preClaimIntakeHookServiceStub{
149			listModels: []agentlib.ModelInfo{
150				{ID: "cheap", Power: 21},
151				{ID: "smart", Power: 94},
152			},
153			finalText: `{"classification":"atomic","confidence":0.99,"reasoning":"single-slice"}`,
154		}
155	
156		hook := NewPreClaimIntakeHook(root, store, intakeHookTestConfig(), svc, nil)
157		got, err := hook(context.Background(), b.ID)
158		require.NoError(t, err)
159		assert.Equal(t, PreClaimIntakeActionableAtomic, got.Outcome)
160		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), atomic.LoadInt32(&svc.executeCalls))
161		assert.Contains(t, svc.lastReq.Prompt, "MODE: intake")
162		assert.Equal(t, root, svc.lastReq.WorkDir)
163		assert.Equal(t, "smart", svc.lastReq.Profile)
164		assert.GreaterOrEqual(t, svc.lastReq.MinPower, 94)
165	}

199	func TestDecompositionHook_SmartProfileUnavailableFallsBackToDefaultProfile(t *testing.T) {
200		root := newPreClaimIntakeHookTestRoot(t)
201		store, b := newPreClaimIntakeHookTestStore(t, root)
202	
203		svc := &preClaimIntakeHookServiceStub{}
204		svc.executeFunc = func(req agentlib.ServiceExecuteRequest) (<-chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, error) {
205			ch := make(chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, 1)
206			if req.Profile == "smart" {
207				ch <- agentlib.ServiceEvent{Type: "final", Data: []byte(`{"status":"error","exit_code":1,"error":"ResolveRoute: no live provider supports prompt of 0 tokens with tools=false at tier ≥ smart"}`)}
208				close(ch)
209				return ch, nil
210			}
211			text := `{"classification":"atomic","confidence":0.99,"reasoning":"fallback-ready"}`
212			ch <- agentlib.ServiceEvent{Type: "final", Data: []byte(`{"status":"success","final_text":` + fmt.Sprintf("%q", text) + `}`)}
213			close(ch)
214			return ch, nil
215		}
216	
217		hook := NewPreClaimIntakeHook(root, store, intakeHookTestConfig(), svc, nil)
218		got, err := hook(context.Background(), b.ID)
219		require.NoError(t, err)
220		assert.Equal(t, PreClaimIntakeActionableAtomic, got.Outcome)
221		assert.Equal(t, int32(2), atomic.LoadInt32(&svc.executeCalls))
222		assert.Equal(t, DefaultRoutingProfile, svc.lastReq.Profile)
223		assert.Zero(t, svc.lastReq.MinPower)
224	}

285			assert.Zero(t, req.MaxPower, "pre-claim intake must not inherit worker max_power")
286			ch := make(chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, 1)
287			ch <- agentlib.ServiceEvent{Type: "final", Data: []byte(`{"status":"success","final_text":"{\"classification\":\"atomic\",\"confidence\":0.99,\"reasoning\":\"frontier-ready\"}"}`)}
288			close(ch)

338		assert.NotContains(t, err.Error(), "empty output")
339	}

341	func TestPreClaimReadiness_DecodesLegacyIntakeJSON(t *testing.T) {
342		tests := []struct {
343			name       string
344			payload    string
345			wantOutcome PreClaimIntakeOutcome
346			wantDetail  string
347		}{
348			{
349				name:        "atomic",
350				payload:     `{"classification":"atomic","confidence":0.99,"reasoning":"single slice"}`,
351				wantOutcome: PreClaimIntakeActionableAtomic,
352				wantDetail:  "single slice",
353			},
354			{
355				name:        "decomposable",
356				payload:     `{"classification":"decomposable","reasoning":"too broad"}`,
357				wantOutcome: PreClaimIntakeTooLargeDecomposed,
358				wantDetail:  "too broad",
359			},
360			{
361				name:        "ambiguous",
362				payload:     `{"classification":"ambiguous","reasoning":"unclear scope"}`,
363				wantOutcome: PreClaimIntakeAmbiguousNeedsHuman,
364				wantDetail:  "unclear scope",
365			},
366			{
367				name:        "rewritten",
368				payload:     `{"classification":"rewritten","reasoning":"safe fix","rewrite":{"changed_fields":["acceptance"],"acceptance":"1. TestFoo\n2. cd cli && go test ./..."}}`,
369				wantOutcome: PreClaimIntakeActionableButRewritten,
370				wantDetail:  "safe fix",
371			},
372		}
373		for _, tt := range tests {
374			t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
375				got, err := decodePreClaimIntakePayloadResult(tt.payload)
376				require.NoError(t, err)
377				assert.Equal(t, tt.wantOutcome, got.Outcome)
378				assert.Equal(t, tt.wantDetail, got.Detail)
379			})
380		}
381	}

383	func TestPreClaimReadiness_DecodesCanonicalReadinessJSON(t *testing.T) {
384		tests := []struct {
385			name        string
386			payload     string
387			wantOutcome PreClaimIntakeOutcome
388			wantDetail  string
389		}{
390			{
391				name:        "actionable_atomic",
392				payload:     `{"outcome":"actionable_atomic","reason":"single slice"}`,
393				wantOutcome: PreClaimIntakeActionableAtomic,
394				wantDetail:  "single slice",
395			},
396			{
397				name:        "too_large_decomposed",
398				payload:     `{"outcome":"too_large_decomposed","reason":"too broad"}`,
399				wantOutcome: PreClaimIntakeTooLargeDecomposed,
400				wantDetail:  "too broad",
401			},
402			{
403				name:        "ambiguous_needs_human",
404				payload:     `{"outcome":"ambiguous_needs_human","reason":"unclear scope"}`,
405				wantOutcome: PreClaimIntakeAmbiguousNeedsHuman,
406				wantDetail:  "unclear scope",
407			},
408			{
409				name:        "readiness_error_fails_open",
410				payload:     `{"outcome":"readiness_error","reason":"skill missing"}`,
411				wantOutcome: PreClaimIntakeError,
412				wantDetail:  "skill missing",
413			},
414			{
415				name:        "system_unready_fails_open",
416				payload:     `{"outcome":"system_unready","reason":"infra failure"}`,
417				wantOutcome: PreClaimIntakeError,
418				wantDetail:  "infra failure",
419			},
420			{
421				name:        "actionable_but_rewritten",
422				payload:     `{"outcome":"actionable_but_rewritten","reason":"needs clarification","rewrite":{"changed_fields":["acceptance"],"description":"","acceptance":"1. TestFoo\n2. cd cli && go test ./..."}}`,
423				wantOutcome: PreClaimIntakeActionableButRewritten,
424				wantDetail:  "needs clarification",
425			},
426			{
427				name:        "detail_field_fallback",
428				payload:     `{"outcome":"actionable_atomic","detail":"via detail field"}`,
429				wantOutcome: PreClaimIntakeActionableAtomic,
430				wantDetail:  "via detail field",
431			},
432		}
433		for _, tt := range tests {
434			t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
435				got, err := decodePreClaimIntakePayloadResult(tt.payload)
436				require.NoError(t, err)
437				assert.Equal(t, tt.wantOutcome, got.Outcome)
438				assert.Equal(t, tt.wantDetail, got.Detail)
439			})
440		}
441	}

443	func TestPreClaimReadiness_UnknownReasonActionableError(t *testing.T) {
444		_, err := decodePreClaimIntakePayloadResult(`{"outcome":"not_a_real_outcome","reason":"some reason"}`)
445		require.Error(t, err)
446		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "not_a_real_outcome")
447		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "actionable_atomic")
448		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "system_unready")
449	}
450	
451	func TestDecompositionHook_ActionableButRewrittenParsesRewrite(t *testing.T) {
452		root := newPreClaimIntakeHookTestRoot(t)
453		store, b := newPreClaimIntakeHookTestStore(t, root)
454		escapedDescription := strings.ReplaceAll(b.Description, "\n", `\n`)
455	
456		svc := &preClaimIntakeHookServiceStub{
457			finalText: `{"classification":"rewritten","confidence":0.91,"reasoning":"safe refinement","rewrite":{"changed_fields":["acceptance","description"],"description":"` + escapedDescription + `\n\nAdd an explicit validation step.","acceptance":"1. TestDecompositionHook_ActionableButRewrittenParsesRewrite\n2. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run \"TestIntake_.*Rewrite|TestLintHook\" -count=1\n3. lefthook run pre-commit"}}`,
458		}
459	
460		hook := NewPreClaimIntakeHook(root, store, intakeHookTestConfig(), svc, nil)
461		got, err := hook(context.Background(), b.ID)
462		require.NoError(t, err)
463		assert.Equal(t, PreClaimIntakeActionableButRewritten, got.Outcome)
464		assert.Equal(t, "safe refinement", got.Detail)
465		assert.Equal(t, []string{"acceptance", "description"}, got.Rewrite.ChangedFields)
466		assert.Contains(t, got.Rewrite.Description, "Add an explicit validation step.")
467		assert.Contains(t, got.Rewrite.Acceptance, "lefthook run pre-commit")
468	}
